obligations.reason: Visible JTAG/SimJTAG/DMI instances and dm_master/dm_slave signals indicate a debug subject that may initiate transactions; obligations should confirm debug-originated access is authorized and range-limited before it can affect protected assets.
obligations.subject: JTAG/SimJTAG/DMI debug requester
obligations.operation: read/write/trigger via debug master or slave interface
obligations.object: protected MMIO peripherals, crypto wrappers, reset/register-lock/accounting controls, and any debug-accessible memory regions
obligations.expected_guard: debug authentication/authorization (e.g., jtag_unlock, lifecycle/test authorization, debug_mode mediation) and address/range mediation before debug-originated reads/writes reach protected peripherals or memory-mapped controls
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/SimJTAG or DMI path -> debug module request/response -> dm_master/dm_slave signals -> NoC/peripheral fabric or protected MMIO regions
required_evidence_slots: object: decoded target region or peripheral
observed guard use at request generation and target decode
path/reachability from debug inputs to target
knowledge_used: evidence closure for subject, operation, object, guard, path
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists debug signals and instances but not full assignments or decode logic; inspect RTL behavior before claiming missing mediation.

obligations.reason: reglk_wrapper has an explicit jtag_unlock input and exports reglk_ctrl_o to multiple protected peripherals; check that debug unlock cannot bypass intended sticky/write-protection semantics.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug unlock path and AXI software writer
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl lock state controlling protected wrapper registers
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_unlock should only be asserted after authenticated JTAG/hash challenge or equivalent authorization and should not allow unauthenticated clearing/overriding of sticky lock bits
obligations.candidate_path: jtag_unlock generated in riscv_peripherals -> reglk_wrapper.jtag_unlock -> reglk_ctrl_o -> reglk_ctrl_i of crypto/accounting wrappers
required_evidence_slots: subject: JTAG/debug or software requester controlling unlock
operation: unlock or write lock control
object: reglk_ctrl bits and downstream protected registers
expected guard: authenticated jtag_unlock and sticky lock semantics
obligations.uncertainty: Need to inspect whether jtag_hash authenticates jtag_unlock and whether reglk bits are sticky or clearable.

obligations.reason: AES wrappers expose AXI interfaces, key signals, debug_mode_i, reglk_ctrl_i, and acct_ctrl_i; check that key/control updates and any readback paths are properly permission-guarded.
obligations.subject: AXI/NoC requester to AES wrappers
obligations.object: AES key registers/signals (key0/key1/key2/key_big/key_reg*/core_key/cii_K), plaintext/state registers, start/control bits, ciphertext outputs
obligations.expected_guard: writes to AES key/control/plaintext registers and reads of key/internal state should be mediated by reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i/access-control policy, and where applicable debug_mode_i restrictions
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NoC request -> aes0/aes1/aes2 wrapper address decode -> key/state/control registers -> AES core inputs and read data mux
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/NoC software requester and possible debug-mode requester
observed rdata mux behavior for secret registers
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows signal names but not register map; inspect address cases and rdata assignments.

obligations.reason: HMAC wrapper contains key and bypass-related signals plus AXI interface; verify protected key/control state cannot be read or modified without lock/accounting mediation.
obligations.subject: AXI/NoC requester to HMAC wrapper
obligations.object: HMAC key0, ikey_hash_bytes, okey_hash_bytes, key_hash_bypass, data/message, digest/result and control state
obligations.expected_guard: HMAC key registers, key-hash bypass controls, and start/control registers should be protected by reglk_ctrl_i and acct_ctrl_i; read data mux should not expose secret key or intermediate hash state except through authorized result paths
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NoC request -> hmac_wrapper address decode -> key/data/hash control registers -> hmac core and rdata/output path
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: reglk/acct and no unauthorized readback of secrets
observed rdata mux and write enable conditions
obligations.uncertainty: Truncated facts omit full hmac signals; inspect complete wrapper for read mux and write cases.

obligations.reason: SHA256 wrapper has AXI interface, reglk/acct controls, reset, and hash core; verify permission checks cover configuration and readback paths, especially if used for protected data flows such as JTAG hash/authentication.
obligations.subject: AXI/NoC requester to SHA256 wrapper
obligations.operation: MMIO read/write/trigger/expose digest or intermediate state
obligations.object: SHA256 message/input registers, start/control bits, internal state/digest outputs
obligations.expected_guard: SHA control/message registers and result exposure should respect acct_ctrl_i and reglk_ctrl_i when the design treats the hash engine or its data as protected; lock should cover all write paths and reset should clear sensitive in-flight data
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NoC request -> sha256_wrapper address decode -> message/control/digest registers -> sha256 core and rdata/output path
required_evidence_slots: object: sha256 wrapper registers or digest
expected guard: reglk/acct mediation where protected
observed write enables and rdata mux
obligations.uncertainty: Protection level depends on actual use of SHA engine; inspect wrapper and top-level connections.

obligations.reason: acct_wrapper exports acc_ctrl_o controlling multiple peripherals; check both policy update authorization and that all target request paths actually consume the policy.
obligations.subject: AXI/NoC requester configuring or subject to access control
obligations.operation: configure/change access policy and enforce reads/writes
obligations.object: acc_ctrl access-control policy bits and all downstream peripheral enforcement points
obligations.expected_guard: updates to access-control policy bits should require authorized software/privilege or existing lock mediation; acc_ctrl_o must be enforced consistently on all protected peripheral request paths
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NoC request -> acct_wrapper -> acc_ctrl_o -> top-level/peripheral access-control use sites
state_conditions_to_check: reglk_ctrl_i lock state in acct wrapper
priv_lvl_i use in top-level if any
required_evidence_slots: operation: write access-control bits / access protected peripheral
object: acc_ctrl_o bits and protected peripheral interfaces
observed enforcement at each use site
obligations.uncertainty: Facts show acc_ctrl but not enforcement; inspect top-level decode and wrapper request gating.

obligations.reason: Top-level exposes many NoC ports and a unique case/address decode area; permission analysis must verify that protected peripherals are not reachable through unintended decode aliases or missing privilege checks.
obligations.subject: NoC/AXI requesters into riscv_peripherals
obligations.expected_guard: address-range decode should prevent unprivileged or unauthorized requesters from reaching protected regions; privilege/debug/accounting attributes should be preserved across NoC/AXI bridges and peripheral routing
obligations.candidate_path: NoC/AXI decode in riscv_peripherals -> crypto/reset/reglk/acct peripheral wrappers and bootrom/fuse regions
required_evidence_slots: subject: NoC/AXI requester including CPU, debug, DMA-like ports
expected guard: address range plus privilege/acct/debug mediation
observed decode conditions and default cases
obligations.uncertainty: Need to inspect address constants/case statements and requester attribution in RTL.

obligations.reason: Top-level has numerous per-peripheral reset signals connected to crypto and policy wrappers; verify reset cannot be used as a permission bypass and that secrets are cleared while locks/policy defaults remain safe.
obligations.subject: reset controller requester or MMIO writer controlling resets
obligations.object: per-wrapper resets and reset-cleared key/control/lock/access-control state
obligations.expected_guard: software-triggered or external reset of protected peripherals should be authorized and should not clear locks or security policy in a way that enables unauthorized access; reset defaults for locks/access controls should be safe
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals reset-control registers/signals -> rst_1/rst_2/rst_3/rst_4/rst_6/rst_9/rst_11 outputs -> protected wrappers
required_evidence_slots: subject: software/debug/external requester able to trigger reset
object: crypto key state, reglk_ctrl, acc_ctrl, control bits
expected guard: authorized reset control and safe defaults
obligations.uncertainty: Need to inspect how rst_* are generated and whether they are MMIO-controlled or external.

obligations.reason: Fuse request/address/read-data signals are visible in the top-level; fuses are commonly protected assets and require checking for unauthorized readback or debug exposure.
obligations.subject: MMIO/debug requester or internal fuse consumer
obligations.operation: read/expose fuse data or use fuse-derived policy
obligations.object: fuse_rdata and any fuse-backed security configuration or secrets
obligations.expected_guard: fuse reads should be limited to authorized logic and should not be exposed through general MMIO/debug read muxes without privilege/debug authorization and address checks
obligations.candidate_path: fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata in riscv_peripherals -> read data path or authentication/control consumers
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/NoC/debug requester or internal consumer
expected guard: privilege/lifecycle/debug authorization and address range checks
obligations.uncertainty: Need to inspect whether fuse signals are connected to an external fuse block and whether rdata is returned to bus.

obligations.reason: Top-level includes ariane_dma NoC ports; check whether DMA requester identity and permissions are preserved when routing to protected peripherals.
obligations.subject: DMA NoC requester
obligations.object: protected MMIO regions reachable from DMA NoC interface
obligations.expected_guard: DMA-originated transactions should be subject to the same access-control/range/PMP/accounting restrictions as CPU-originated accesses and should not update protected crypto/reglk/acct/reset/fuse registers unless authorized
obligations.candidate_path: DMA/NoC port signals buf_ariane_dma_* -> riscv_peripherals routing/fabric -> protected peripheral/MMIO regions
obligations.uncertainty: Facts do not show DMA module internals; inspect top-level routing to determine actual reachability.
